issues.display_title: Issue #9: Fix Issue Pressing F5 Did Not Work in File Manager
issues.main_description_html:
Demo by developer

Screen.Recording.2026-06-23.at.15.57.53.mov

Pressing F5 does not refresh the directory listing in the File Manager. The keyboard shortcut appears to be unbound or not wired to the refresh action.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. Pressing F5 while the File Manager is focused refreshes the current directory listing.
  2. The refresh reflects files added/removed on disk since the last load.

📊 Product analysis context (from docs/projectbase/analyze.md)

Priority: 🟡 Medium — Quick win.

Reach Impact Confidence Effort Verdict
Med Med (1×) High ~0.25 pm Quick win — cheapest of the three

Engineering hypothesis: Cheap keybinding wiring — F5 appears unbound or not mapped to the File Manager refresh action. Restores expected desktop behavior. Confirm the hotkey is registered through the existing settings/hotkeys path rather than hardcoded.

issues.display_title: Issue #8: Handle Issue File Sorting by "Name" is Not Respected for .pts Files
issues.main_description_html:
Demo by developer

2026-06-23.17-47-16.mp4

A bug has been identified in the file manager UI where sorting by "Name" does not correctly apply to all file types.

When a user clicks the "Name" column header to sort files alphabetically (ascending), the .jpg files sort correctly at the top. However, the .pts files are entirely segregated and pushed to the bottom of the grid, failing to interleave alphabetically with the rest of the files. It appears the system is either applying an unintended primary sort by file extension, or the .pts files are completely bypassing the alphabetical sorting logic.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. When the "Name" column is set to ascending/descending, all files in the directory sort strictly alphabetically by their filenames.
  2. .pts files are not artificially grouped at the bottom; they must appear in their correct alphabetical position alongside .jpg files.
  3. Toggling the sort direction updates the order correctly for all file extensions.

📊 Product analysis context (from docs/projectbase/analyze.md)

Priority: 🟡 Medium — Quick win.

Reach Impact Confidence Effort Verdict
Med Med (1×) High ~0.5 pm Quick win

Engineering hypothesis: Comparator bug — the sort is likely applying an unintended primary sort by file extension (or .pts bypasses the alphabetical comparator entirely). Logic is isolated, so the fix is contained. Ensure the comparator sorts strictly by filename across all extensions and that toggling direction reverses uniformly (AC #1–#3).

issues.display_title: Issue #4: Fix Bug Output Path Retains Previous Directory Instead of Current Active Folder
issues.main_description_html:
Demo by developer

Screen.Recording.2026-06-23.at.16.01.19.mov

Description

A state management issue was identified in the processing-tools UI (e.g. Auto Level).

When a user navigates to a new folder in the File Manager and opens a processing tool, the "Output" path field does not automatically update to reflect the newly selected folder. Instead, it caches and displays the directory path used during the previous editing session.

Expected Behavior

The "Output" path should dynamically sync with the File Manager's active directory, so by default processed images are saved in the current source folder — unless the user explicitly chooses a custom output location.

Context / Reference

Grounded in docs/feedback 05-06-2026/analyze-new.md (§3 Edit suite). The Output field is shared across the Edit processing tools (Auto Level, Minify, Scale Up, Nadir, etc.), so the fix should apply consistently wherever an Output/Browse row appears. Part of the broader File-Manager / Edit state-sync theme.

Investigation directions

  • The Output path is likely seeded once (cached in tool state/Redux) and not re-synced to the File Manager's current directory when the tool opens / the active folder changes.
  • On tool open (and on active-directory change), default Output to the current source folder unless the user has explicitly set a custom path in this session.
  • Verify this across all Edit tools that expose Output + Browse, and that an explicit custom selection is preserved (not clobbered) while switching files within the same session.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. Navigating to a new folder and opening a processing tool automatically sets the Output path to match the current active folder.
  2. The application no longer forces the user to manually browse and correct the output path after switching folders.
  3. An explicit, user-chosen custom output location is respected and not overwritten by the auto-sync.

Testing Scenario (for Tester)

Pre-conditions: Build with the fix · at least two folders with images.

TC1 — Output Syncs to Active Folder (primary)

  1. Open Folder A, open a processing tool (e.g. Auto Level) — note the Output.
  2. Navigate to Folder B and open a processing tool again.
  3. Expected: Output now reflects Folder B (the current active folder), not the previous session's path.

TC2 — Default Save Location

  1. Run a job without choosing a custom output.
  2. Expected: Processed images save to the current source folder by default.

TC3 — Custom Output Respected

  1. Explicitly Browse and set a custom output, then run a job.
  2. Expected: Output uses the custom path; auto-sync does not override an explicit choice during that session.

TC4 — Cross-Tool Consistency

  1. Repeat TC1 across several Edit tools that expose Output + Browse.
  2. Expected: Same correct sync behavior everywhere.

issues.display_title: Issue #5: Migrate Discontinued Imagen 4.0 Endpoints to Gemini 3.1 Flash
issues.main_description_html:
Demo by developer

Description

We received a deprecation notice regarding the image-generation endpoints used by our Windows and macOS desktop apps. The imagen-4.0 family is being discontinued and must be migrated to gemini-3.1-flash-image to prevent the app's image-generation features from breaking.

Migration Mapping

Endpoint to be discontinued Recommended migration path
imagen-4.0-generate-001 gemini-3.1-flash-image
imagen-4.0-ultra-generate-001 gemini-3.1-flash-image
imagen-4.0-fast-generate-001 gemini-3.1-flash-image

Implications:

  1. The migration likely requires rebuilding/updating the ai-image-gen binary (its model is internal) and/or passing the new model name to it from TS — coordinate with the binary owner.
  2. The exact three endpoints from the notice (*-generate-001, *-ultra-*, *-fast-*) don't appear literally in the binary; what's present is imagen-4.0-upscale-preview. Confirm the precise mapping with the binary owner so no usage is missed.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. All references to imagen-4.0-generate-001, imagen-4.0-ultra-generate-001, and imagen-4.0-fast-generate-001 (and any imagen-4.0-*) are completely removed from the desktop application — including the bundled ai-image-gen binary, not just the TS codebase.
  2. Image generation works on the compiled Windows app using the new endpoint.
  3. Image generation works on the compiled macOS app using the new endpoint.
  4. No degradation in generation speed or feature accessibility observed by the user.

Testing Scenario (for Tester)

Pre-conditions: Compiled builds (Win + mac) with the updated binary · a valid AI key configured.

TC1 — Windows generation

  1. Run AI Nadir Patching / Virtual Staging / Room Staging on Windows.
  2. Expected: Generation succeeds via gemini-3.1-flash-image; no imagen-4.0 endpoint errors.

TC2 — macOS generation

  1. Repeat TC1 on macOS.
  2. Expected: Succeeds identically.

TC3 — No legacy references

  1. Search source and inspect the shipped ai-image-gen binary strings.
  2. Expected: No imagen-4.0-* endpoint remains.

TC4 — Performance / parity

  1. Compare generation time + output quality vs. previous.
  2. Expected: No regression in speed or feature accessibility.

issues.display_title: Issue #11: Handle Enfuse Process Interruption on Screen Navigation
issues.main_description_html:
Demo by developer

2026-06-23.17-54-08.mp4

Description

An issue has been reported where the Enfuse process halts or stops working if the user navigates away from the active screen.

Crucial Requirement: As explicitly requested, all investigations and implementations for this issue must be done exclusively on the new Rust + Tauri build. Do not apply this to the old legacy build (Electron).

We need to address this either by fixing the underlying architecture so it runs in the background, or by implementing a preventative UX measure to warn the user.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. The code changes are implemented strictly on the new Rust + Tauri repository.
  2. Either the Enfuse process successfully continues running in the background when the user navigates to a different screen, OR a clear warning modal is displayed preventing the user from accidentally leaving the screen and interrupting the task.

issues.display_title: Issue #7: Fix Issue Scroll Position Resets to Top on Add/Delete in Enfuse (Auto Stitching) List
issues.main_description_html:
Demo by developer

2026-06-23.17-51-05.mp4

Description

A highly disruptive UX issue has been reported within the Enfuse (Auto Stitching) feature. Whenever a user performs an action that updates the list—specifically adding a new image or deleting an existing 360 image—the UI forcibly resets the user's scroll position to the very top of the page.

This causes severe friction for users working with large datasets. For example, a user managing a massive stitching job with 15,000 images reported extreme frustration, as they have to manually scroll all the way back down to their previous location after every single deletion.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. When a user deletes an image from the Enfuse list, the image is successfully removed, and the user's scroll position remains completely stationary.
  2. When a user adds an image to the Enfuse list, the scroll position does not jump to the top.
  3. The scroll preservation works consistently, even when managing extremely large stitching jobs (e.g., thousands of images).

📊 Product analysis context (from docs/projectbase/analyze.md)

Priority: 🔴 High — highest RICE of the open defects.

Reach Impact Confidence Effort Verdict
High (all heavy users) High (2×) High ~0.5 pm Quick win — ship first

Why it matters: Maps to the high-volume power-user persona — the cohort feeling the most friction and the clearest near-term retention risk. The 15k-image scenario makes this severe daily friction.

Engineering hypothesis: Likely a list-key / virtualization issue (the grid uses @tanstack/react-virtual). Suspect a non-stable React key or full list remount on add/delete that drops the virtualizer's scroll offset.

Acceptance bar = large-dataset stability, not just "it scrolls": Treat parity-at-scale as a cutover-grade gate. Verify scroll stays stationary on add and delete at thousands of items, per AC #3.

issues.display_title: Issue #10: [Audit] Tauri App Feature Validation & Cross-check
issues.main_description_html:
Goal

Validate that every migrated feature in the Tauri v2 build behaves at parity with the original Electron app, and cross-check the migration inventory against what is actually implemented and working.

Scope

Full feature sweep across the app, cross-referenced with docs/migration/00-ipc-inventory.md (142 IPC channels) and the 11 per-feature docs in docs/migration/features/.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. Each feature area is validated against its docs/migration/features/*.md contract and marked pass/fail.
  2. Any parity gaps or regressions are filed as linked follow-up issues.
  3. The IPC inventory status (✅ / 🔵 / ⬜) is reconciled with actual implementation state.

Context: see docs/projectbase/analyze.md — the project is in a stabilization/cutover phase; this audit feeds Phase 4 cutover readiness.

Completed

  • Optimized Browse
  • Fixed Copy & Move in File Manager
  • Image Centering
  • Upload to GoThru
  • Upload to Panorra
  • Upload to StreetView
  • Fixed error in Enfuse
  • Duplicate feature
  • Optimized Sorting
  • Copy Path in File Manager
  • Create Folder
  • Fixed flickering on Browse
  • Subcription
  • Clean up code on some scripts

issues.display_title: Issue #2: Fix Bug UI Freezes and Processing Halts During Massive Batch Enfuse Job
issues.main_description_html:
Demo by developer

2026-06-23.18-05-28.mp4

Description

A critical performance bug was reported by a user attempting to process a very large batch of images (4,536 images) using the Enfuse feature.

Issue Details

  1. The user initiated an enfuse job for the entire batch.
  2. The processing engine successfully enfused the very first image and saved it to disk.
  3. However, the app's Queue UI failed to update (Success counter stayed at 0, and the item stayed stuck on Queued).
  4. The entire application became completely unresponsive — none of the interactive UI buttons worked, forcing the user to force-quit the app.
  5. Visual feedback (the rotating blue loading indicator) kept looping, indicating the UI thread is locked or overwhelmed without technically crashing the OS window.

This strongly indicates the heavy image processing is either blocking the main/UI thread, causing a memory issue when loading thousands of file paths, or the queue manager is failing to catch the success callback from the processing engine to trigger the next item.

Context / Reference

Grounded in docs/feedback 05-06-2026/analyze-new.md (§3 Edit suite / §8): Enfuse is a native-binary-backed batch Edit tool. Same family as the other queue/throughput issues — see #1407 (AutoLevel slow save: strictly-sequential queue, per-file process churn). At 4,536 items the queue/state model likely degrades non-linearly.

Investigation directions

  • UI-thread blocking: ensure batch orchestration + per-item state updates are not synchronously processing the full 4.5k list on the renderer/main thread; yield/stream instead of building giant arrays.
  • Success callback wiring: confirm the per-item success event from the Enfuse engine actually updates the queue (counter + status) and triggers the next item — the "1 done but counter=0/Queued" symptom points here.
  • Memory: loading thousands of file paths/objects at once — paginate/virtualize the queue list; avoid retaining buffers.
  • Responsiveness: Stop All / Cancel must stay clickable (don't block the event loop); process incrementally.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. The application can successfully queue and process massive batches (e.g. 5,000 images) without locking up the UI.
  2. Interactive elements (especially Stop All / Cancel) remain fully clickable and responsive while a heavy batch job runs in the background.
  3. The queue accurately updates the Success counter and visual status for each item as soon as it is written to disk.

Testing Scenario (for Tester)

Pre-conditions: Build with the fix · a folder with a very large batch (≥ ~4,500 images) · note machine CPU/RAM/OS.

TC1 — Massive Batch Responsiveness (primary)

  1. Queue the full large batch in Enfuse and start.
  2. Expected: UI stays responsive; counters/status update per item as each completes; no freeze/force-quit.

TC2 — Stop/Cancel Under Load

  1. While the batch is running, click Stop All / Cancel an item.
  2. Expected: Buttons respond promptly; processing stops/cancels cleanly.

TC3 — Accurate Per-Item Status

  1. Watch the first several items complete.
  2. Expected: Success counter increments and each row flips Queued → Processing → Success in sync with files appearing on disk (no stuck-on-Queued).

TC4 — Memory / Long-Run Stability

  1. Let the batch run for an extended period.
  2. Expected: No runaway memory growth or progressive slowdown; completes or remains cancellable throughout.

issues.display_title: Issue #3: Fix Bug "Retry" Action Missing for Canceled Enfuse Items & Queue Management Logic
issues.main_description_html:
Demo by developer

2026-06-23.18-01-40.mp4

Description

The Enfuse (and Edit queue) per-item actions are inconsistent across statuses. Users can't easily re-queue items that Canceled or Failed, a possibly-redundant Retry appears on Success items, and the remove (X) action on Success items doesn't reliably clear them.

Context / Reference

Grounded in docs/feedback 05-06-2026/analyze-new.md (§3 Edit suite). Part of the Edit-queue robustness family — relates to #1435 (massive Enfuse batch freeze) and the queue/status handling in the AutoLevel/Minify/Enfuse tools. Per-item controls (Retry / Remove / Cancel) should behave consistently by status across the Edit queues.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. Canceled and Failed items show a Retry action that re-queues the item for processing.
  2. The Retry action on Success items is removed (or justified) so the per-item controls aren't confusing.
  3. The X (remove) action reliably clears Success items from the queue list.
  4. Per-item controls behave consistently across statuses (Queued / Processing / Success / Failed / Canceled).

Testing Scenario (for Tester)

Pre-conditions: Build with the fix · a queue that can produce mixed statuses (some succeed, some fail/canceled).

TC1 — Retry on Canceled/Failed

  1. Run a batch; cancel one item and let another fail.
  2. Expected: Both show a Retry (circular arrow); clicking it re-queues the item and it processes again.

TC2 — Success item controls

  1. Inspect a Success item's actions.
  2. Expected: No confusing/redundant Retry (per decision); the X removes it from the list immediately.

TC3 — Consistency across statuses

  1. Review actions on Queued, Processing, Success, Failed, Canceled rows.
  2. Expected: Controls are consistent and sensible per status; no dead buttons.
